frames.name: Corporate resistance to military overreach
frames.description: Presents Anthropic as standing up against Pentagon pressure to remove ethical AI restrictions, with the company defending against potential misuse in autonomous weapons and mass surveillance
frames.emphasis: Anthropic's objections to allowing 'all lawful use' and concerns about lethal autonomous weapons and mass surveillance

frames.name: Presidential authority vs. tech company obstruction
frames.description: Frames the conflict as Trump asserting government authority against a 'leftwing' tech company that is trying to 'strong-arm' the military
frames.emphasis: Trump's characterization of Anthropic as 'leftwing nut jobs' making a 'disastrous mistake' by trying to control military AI use

frames.name: Silicon Valley's evolving relationship with defense
frames.description: Contextualizes the dispute within the broader trend of tech companies moving from avoiding defense work to embracing military contracts
frames.emphasis: The transformation from tech companies 'largely avoiding defense work to increasingly embracing it and eventually becoming full-blown military contractors'

outlierPerspectives: Worker solidarity across AI companies - hundreds of OpenAI and Google employees signing letters supporting Anthropic's position
Anthropic as the only AI company currently working with classified military systems, making it uniquely positioned in this conflict
missingContext: Specific details about what 'all lawful use' would permit that current restrictions prevent
The exact nature and scope of current AI restrictions that the Pentagon wants removed
Financial implications of the ban for both Anthropic and government operations
Timeline and specifics of the 'weeks' of clashes mentioned
Details about how other AI companies (Google, OpenAI, xAI) are responding to similar Pentagon pressure
Anthropic's public response or statement to Trump's announcement
Legal framework governing AI company contracts with the military
